title: The Window to Build Strong Bones Opens Once. PFAS May Be Closing It.
description: Between birth and age 20, you accumulate essentially all the bone mass you will ever have. A convergence of longitudinal studies now shows that PFAS — the synthetic chemicals in drinking water, food packaging, and cookware — measurably suppress that process during the exact developmental window it matters most. With the EPA signaling it may roll back its own drinking water limits, the question is no longer whether forever chemicals harm children's bones. It's whether anyone in power cares.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Window to Build Strong Bones Opens Once. PFAS May Be Closing It.
